answer: Argon2id with parameters 64M/3/4 (about 64 MB memory, 3 iterations, parallelism 4).
Refresh tokens expire in 7 days and are rotated on use (refresh(7d-rotate)).
The Notification Service runs on port 9200.
RDS PostgreSQL 15.4 is running now, upgraded from 14.9.
API Gateway throttles are 5000 burst and 2000 steady (5000b/2000s).
Argon2id was chosen over bcrypt for better side-channel resistance.
Drizzle is used instead of Prisma for a zero-dependency approach.
Portfolio and Notification validate RS256 JWTs via the shared JWKS verification path (all-svc + Lambda-JWKS), with JWKS caching (LRU 1000 entries/5 min).
Rate limiting is at ALB to absorb DDoS traffic before requests reach containers/app code.
Client request -> API Gateway schema validation -> Lambda JWKS claims validation -> VPC Link -> ALB rate limit -> Portfolio endpoint -> RBAC role hierarchy check -> rebalance allowed/denied.

If JWKS is down, auth verification fails across all dependent services and the Lambda authorizer (down=all-fail). Token issuance can continue because signing uses a local private key. Grace is limited to cached JWKS entries (LRU 1000/5 min).
Real-time portfolio alerting depends on planned WebSocket integration between Notification and Gateway; status is planned for Q3, not active yet.
Yes, the UTC+13/+14 expiry issue is addressed by using Unix timestamps for token time logic (tz-UTC13-14(unix-ts)).

Use the existing webhook subsystem for auth events with exponential backoff retry (5 attempts over 24h), routed through current service/security controls. Enterprise-specific webhook auth/signature contract details are NOT IN CONTEXT.
With pool20 per Auth instance, 5 instances can open up to 100 simultaneous DB connections.

The pool20 limit caps DB concurrency during tax-lot Celery work, helping prevent DB connection exhaustion; the Celery unbounded-memory issue can still cause worker instability/backlog and is still under investigation.
No. Spot is marked Spot-ONLY-batch, so Auth (online critical path) should not run on Fargate Spot.
The stateless RS256/JWKS multi-service verification doctrine (multi-svc-verify) conflicts with in-memory session caches in Auth.
30-day retention defines how long restore points are kept, while RPO 1h/RTO 4h define recovery objectives (up to 1 hour data loss, restore within 4 hours, us-west-2 DR).
The RDS upgrade required enforcing TLS verification in the connection string: sslmode=verify-full with the global CA bundle.
tax_lots columns: id, account_id, security_id, acquisition_date, cost_basis, quantity, adjusted, created_at.
After password verification, login flow requires MFA TOTP before token issuance; only then are RS256 access (15m) and refresh (7d rotated) tokens issued.
10 recovery codes are generated, and each code is single-use.
Webhook retries use exponential backoff with 5 attempts over 24 hours.

At rest: passwords hashed with Argon2id (64M/3/4) and PII encrypted with per-tenant AES-256-GCM. In transit: mTLS via Envoy plus DB TLS with sslmode=verify-full and global bundle. Auth/session controls include RS256/JWKS token verification, MFA TOTP, and secrets rotation every 90 days.

Gateway schema validation -> Lambda JWKS/claims stage -> VPC Link -> ALB (1000/min/IP) -> Auth handlePostLogin -> Argon2id password verify -> MFA TOTP challenge/verify -> issue RS256 access token (15m) + rotated refresh token (7d).

Billing should follow shared patterns: integrate behind gateway chain (schema -> Lambda JWKS -> VPC Link -> ALB), use RS256/JWKS verification, ALB-first rate limiting, mTLS/SSL security controls, RFC7807+traceId errors, handle{Verb}{Resource} naming, and GHA->ECR->ECS blue/green deployment. Rust framework choice is NOT IN CONTEXT.

At 1500 req/min from one IP: gateway-level throttle likely does not trigger first (limit shown as 5000 burst/2000 steady), then ALB per-IP limit (1000/min/IP) throttles excess. About 1000/min can reach Auth path; about 500/min are blocked at ALB before container handling.

JWT signing-key dependency map: Auth service (signs with local private key), JWKS publication/lookup path, Lambda JWKS claims validator at gateway, and all JWT-verifying services (including Portfolio and Notification). If JWKS is unavailable, verification fails broadly even though Auth can keep issuing tokens.

Commit triggers GitHub Actions pipeline: lint -> typecheck -> test -> build -> push image to ECR -> deploy to ECS. ClaudeCode review is part of process, rollout is blue/green behind ALB for zero downtime, and Spot capacity is reserved for batch workloads only.
